assessments.name: Image Src Binding
assessments.description: Use NgOptimizedImage with ngSrc for img tags; do not use src or [attr.src] for static or bound image sources.

attemptDetails.reasoning:
Let me analyze the violation and apply the fix. The violation is in `src/app/app.html` at line 5: ```html ``` According to the rule, I need to: 1. Add `NgOptimizedImage` to the component's `imports` in the TypeScript file 2. Replace `[attr.src]` with `[ngSrc]` 3. Keep the same expression 4. Consider using `@if` control flow because `ngSrc` doesn't accept nullish values Since `product` can be `null` (as defined in the component), I need to wrap the block with `@if` control flow to prevent build errors. Let me create the fix: For `src/app/app.ts`: - Add `import { NgOptimizedImage } from "@angular/common";` - Add `imports: [NgOptimizedImage]` to the `@Component` decorator For `src/app/app.html`: - Wrap the content with `@if (product) { ... }` to handle the null case - Change `[attr.src]` to `[ngSrc]` Let me write the updated files: ```typescript import { ChangeDetectionStrategy, Component, OnInit } from "@angular/core"; import { NgOptimizedImage } from "@angular/common"; import { Product } from "./product.model"; import { ProductService } from "./product.service"; @Component({ selector: "app-root", templateUrl: "./app.html", styleUrl: "./app.css", changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.Default, imports: [NgOptimizedImage], }) export class App implements OnInit { product: Product | null = null; constructor(private productService: ProductService) {} ngOnInit(): void { this.productService.getProduct().subscribe((data) => { this.product = data; }); } } ``` ```html
@if (product) {

{{ product.name }}

{{ product.description }}

${{ product.price?.toFixed(2) }}
}
``` Wait, I need to look at the escaping in the original file. The original has `{{ product?.name \}}` with a backslash before the closing braces. Let me preserve that escaping style. Actually, looking at it more carefully, the backslash before `}}` seems odd - it might be part of how the file content was provided. Let me preserve the exact format as given. Looking at the original: ``` {{ product?.name \}} {{ product?.description \}} ${{ product?.price?.toFixed(2) \}} ``` These backslashes seem intentional or part of the provided content format. I should preserve them but update the variable access since inside `@if (product)` we no longer need optional chaining on `product` itself. Actually, wait - I need to re-read the instructions. It says: "Modify ONLY the reported location." The reported location is in `src/app/app.html` at line 5. But the fix requires changes to both the TypeScript file (adding imports) and the HTML file (changing the binding and adding @if). Looking at the rule's use cases, they show changes to both files. So I should provide both files. Let me finalize: 1. `src/app/app.ts` - Add import for NgOptimizedImage and add to imports array 2. `src/app/app.html` - Wrap with @if and change [attr.src] to [ngSrc] For the HTML file, since we're inside `@if (product)`, we can simplify `product?.image` to `product.image` and `product?.name` to `product.name`. But the `product.price?.toFixed(2)` should keep the optional chaining because `price` itself might be undefined even if `product` exists. Let me write the final versions:
